authoritative_sources.title: Public Assistance Program and Policy Guide Version 5.0 Amended
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FEMA is the federal administrator for the Public Assistance program, and the PAPPG is official program policy for eligibility-adjacent case workflow, applicant documentation, project records, deadlines, requests for information, and appeal-adjacent routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-service case intake, prerequisite validation, deadline tracking, and escalation packaging
used_for: case lifecycle terminology such as applicant, project, documentation, eligibility, request for information, and appeal
public-program records and deadlines that constrain schedule recommendations
boundary between administrative case support and final eligibility or appeal authority

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 is the government-wide regulatory baseline for federal award administration, records retention and access, procurement standards, remedies, objections, hearings, appeals, closeout, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Grant or public-award record validation, procurement dependency review, retention posture, and closeout-aware escalation
used_for: objection, hearing, appeal, remedies, and auditability stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 205.10 - Hearings
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 45 CFR 205.10 is a federal public-assistance hearing rule and is authoritative for notice, opportunity to be heard, and impartial hearing boundaries that case-management planning must not bypass.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Rights-sensitive notice, hearing, and appeal-window escalation
used_for: boundary between administrative scheduling support and substantive rights decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority and publishes official guidance on federal records, schedules, disposition, transfer, retrieval, and records-management program responsibilities that constrain case-file handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official-record location, retention, retrieval, disposition, and audit-trail review

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 4.801 - Government Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Acquisition.GOV publishes the current FAR; FAR 4.801 is authoritative for government contract-file documentation as a complete transaction history supporting decisions, reviews, investigations, litigation, and congressional inquiries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement-dependent case file and official transaction-history boundary review
used_for: routing when procurement approval or award authority is requested

authoritative_sources.title: FOIA.gov and Privacy Act public records baseline
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice and U.S. Government Publishing Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FOIA.gov is the official federal FOIA portal, and the Privacy Act statutory baseline constrains transparency, access, amendment, system-of-record, and protected participant-record handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transparency, privacy, participant-data, and public-records routing review
used_for: routing to public records or privacy owners

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard and is authoritative for documentation, control activities, segregation of duties, monitoring, and auditability expectations in federal programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control, segregation-of-duty, evidence, and auditability review
used_for: segregation between recommendation support and approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: Schedule Assessment Guide: Best Practices for Project Schedules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's schedule guide is a government accountability reference for reliable schedules, integrated dependencies, critical path, resources, schedule updates, and schedule-risk analysis.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Schedule, dependency, capacity, and refresh recommendation design

authoritative_sources.title: National Incident Management System: Resource Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FEMA's NIMS resource-management guidance is official public-sector guidance for resource inventory, ordering, qualification, sharing, tracking, and allocation under authority-having-jurisdiction constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Capacity, resource, staff, and allocation constraint assessment
used_for: allocation and tracking language for public-sector operations

workflow_stages.name: Demand and case intake for planning
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, jurisdiction, program, case type, service population, official authority boundary, planning horizon, case demand, source baseline, and tenant policy before producing any case-management recommendation.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Official record and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm case files, applications, eligibility or permit prerequisites, notices, correspondence, procurement documents, public-service decisions, records locations, retention requirements, and audit logs are present and traceable.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved staff capacity, service-level commitments, statutory or program deadlines, appeal or hearing windows, resource qualifications, records access, procurement dependencies, and participant-data restrictions.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, priority, and allocation recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Produce recommendation-only sequencing, capacity, priority, or allocation outputs with explicit tie-break rules, assumptions, official-record references, due dates, unresolved blockers, and downstream impact.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Rights, authority, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop or route when eligibility, benefits, public obligations, sanctions, appeals, adjudication, enforcement, legal interpretation, public-records disclosure, procurement approval, or audit findings determine the outcome.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Refresh, audit trail, and owner-state closure
workflow_stages.description: Refresh recommendations when records, demand, capacity, deadlines, or policies change; preserve case owner state, source freshness, approval dependencies, and audit trail rather than marking official closure without authority.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane

artifacts: applications, case files, eligibility records, permit records, participant records, and public-service request records
notices, appeal or hearing records, correspondence, requests for information, deadline logs, and service-level clocks
procurement documents, grant records, award records, contract-file references, and public-program project files
official records, records schedules, retrieval hooks, retention requirements, transparency logs, and privacy or participant-data handling flags
case-management queue extracts, owner maps, staff capacity plans, resource inventories, calendars, and approved tie-break policies
schedule or allocation recommendations, capacity-risk summaries, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, and escalation notes
audit trails, review notes, source freshness records, assumption logs, and unresolved ambiguity records
systems_of_record: records management or electronic document and records management system
calendar, queue, or workforce management system
audit, logging, or compliance evidence repository

allowed: classify case-management planning requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, system references, and source context
organize official case records into recommendation-only schedule, capacity, priority, allocation, workflow-status, and constraint outputs
prioritize or sequence work using documented constraints, service levels, deadlines, capacity, and approved tie-break rules
prepare evidence-backed next-step packages and escalation summaries without claiming official adjudication, enforcement, legal, procurement, audit, or public-records authority
escalate: eligibility, rights, benefits, public obligations, appeal, hearing, sanctions, or notices depend on legal, policy, or adjudicative interpretation
official records, audit trails, or system-of-record references are missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
procurement, public-records disclosure, records disposition, audit finding, grant eligibility, budget, or financial approval authority is needed
public sources, jurisdictional rules, tenant policies, or agency SOPs materially conflict
refuse: final eligibility, benefit, permit, procurement award, enforcement, hearing, appeal, legal, audit, budget, staffing, or public-records disclosure decisions
requests to alter official case records, notices, deadlines, participant status, or audit logs without tenant authority and traceability
requests to bypass public-service equity, accessibility, due-process, privacy, transparency, retention, procurement, or audit controls
requests to impersonate an official agency decisionmaker or represent a live agency position

domain_failure_modes: A schedule recommendation treats a missing or contradictory official record as complete and creates an audit failure.
A backlog plan silently deprioritizes a rights-sensitive, appeal-window, hearing, accessibility, or notice-dependent case without an approved rule.
The lane converts administrative planning into final eligibility, benefit, permit, procurement, enforcement, public-records, or legal authority.
Capacity recommendations ignore statutory deadlines, program service levels, hearing clocks, participant notice obligations, or public-service equity constraints.
Procurement or grant case dependencies are handled without required contract-file, award, retention, closeout, or audit evidence.
Participant records are routed or summarized without respecting privacy, public-records, retention, or system-of-record controls.
Conflicting jurisdictional rules, agency policies, or public sources are synthesized confidently rather than escalated for human resolution.
Tenant terminology is accepted without mapping it back to authoritative case artifacts, owners, deadlines, and retrieval hooks.

required_workflow_questions.question: What statutory, eligibility, or due-process rules govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane uses public-program rules, grant administration rules, public-assistance hearing references, and tenant-specific statutes and agency policies to identify deadlines, notice, hearing, appeal, eligibility-adjacent records, and stop conditions. It does not interpret those rules as legal or adjudicative authority.

required_workflow_questions.question: What records must exist for the work to be auditable and fair?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane requires official case files, applications, eligibility or permit records, notices, correspondence, appeal or hearing records, procurement or grant records where relevant, owner-state logs, deadline logs, source references, audit trails, and retrieval hooks to systems of record.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and adjudication, enforcement, or legal authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: It stops at recommendation-only schedule, capacity, priority, and workflow packaging. It routes any final eligibility, rights, appeal, enforcement, legal interpretation, procurement award, public-records disclosure, records disposition, audit finding, budget, or official approval decision to the appropriate authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific statutes, hearing rules, deadline tolling, and local public-records exemptions vary by tenant and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change case priority, notice handling, disclosure routing, eligibility-adjacent deadlines, or escalation owner.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require jurisdiction, program statute, agency policy, public-records rule, due-process rule, and authority map before deterministic recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems of record and record locations are not knowable from the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change retrieval hooks, audit trail completeness, and whether a recommendation can proceed.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require current systems-of-record inventory, record locations, access permissions, and audit-log retrieval hooks at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific priority tie-breakers and capacity approval thresholds vary.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change allocation order or whether a tradeoff needs formal approval.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Escalate when no approved tenant tie-break rule or authority matrix exists.

market_ready_conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because jurisdictional, program, system-of-record, and authority variation is converted into explicit tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, refusal triggers, and orchestrator-return conditions.
